title: rubus serpens subsp. flaccidifolius
intro: Rubus serpens subsp. flaccidifolius, also known as the flexible-leaved dewberry, is a subspecies of the dewberry plant, which belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Rubus serpens subsp. flaccidifolius include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and protect against infections, as well as its antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and has also been used to make medicinal extracts and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
